steps.title: Express the obtuse angle
steps.content:
The acute angle is $(9x-490)^\circ$. Since each acute angle and adjacent obtuse angle are supplementary, an obtuse angle has measure:
$$180-(9x-490)=670-9x$$

steps.title: Match the given sum form
steps.content:
The sum of one acute angle and three obtuse angles is:
$$(9x-490)+3(670-9x)=9x-490+2010-27x=-18x+1520$$

steps.title: Compare constants
steps.content: The problem says this sum is $(-18x+w)^\circ$. Since the simplified expression is $-18x+1520$, the constant term is $w=1520$. The answer is 1520.
